description: Aggregate index generated from papers/<PaperName>/status.json. Paper-local status files are the source of truth for status, human summaries, review rows, and PaperInterface metadata.
review_count_policy: reviewed_rows counts saved human dashboard rows tracked in the public repository. total_rows counts the current dashboard review surface from each paper-local status.json. Agent source audits are not counted as human review.
paper_interface_maintenance_policy: PaperInterface.lean should stay compact and source-facing. Broad proof aliases belong in ProofInterface.lean or implementation modules.

papers.title: Addressing Discretization-Induced Bias in Demographic Prediction
papers.authors: Evan Dong, Aaron Schein, Yixin Wang, and Nikhil Garg
papers.main_caveat: Main theorem route differs from the paper proof in places; the validation report records the proof-route deviation.

human_review.source: paper-local status.json review_surface; human entries come from dashboard logs

papers.title: Internet Advertising and the Generalized Second-Price Auction
papers.authors: Benjamin Edelman, Michael Ostrovsky, and Michael Schwarz
papers.source_version: NBER Working Paper 11765 / AER 2007 publication citation
papers.main_caveat: GSP non-truthfulness, running example, Theorem 7, and finite Theorem 8 source-extensive endpoints are exposed; the remaining source-completion boundary is explicit in the README.
papers.human_summary: Non-truthfulness, examples, and finite theorem endpoints are exposed with an explicit completion boundary.

papers.main_caveat: Recommendation fairness propositions and theorem statements are closed under the formal source model.

papers.title: Competitive Auctions and Digital Goods
papers.authors: Andrew V. Goldberg, Jason D. Hartline, and Andrew Wright
papers.source_version: SODA 2001 / InterTrust numbering crosswalk; journal version controls refinements
papers.main_caveat: Theorem 8.2 is formalized against the journal version's monotone truthful randomized-auction statement; Lean refutes the broader technical-report wording with a two-bidder 101 > 100 source-audit counterexample. Theorem 9.3 is closed against the paper's focused set-of-bids deterministic auction convention.
papers.human_summary: Theorem 8.2 follows the journal version; the broader preliminary wording is documented as refuted.

human_review.source: paper-local status.json review_surface; human entries come from dashboard logs

papers.title: Dropping Standardized Testing for Admissions Trades Off Information and Access
papers.authors: Nikhil Garg, Hannah Li, and Faidra Monachou
papers.main_caveat: Lemma 1, Proposition 1, Lemma 2, Theorems 1-2, and selected Section 5 bridge endpoints are exposed; concrete Bayesian-game row identifications and later strategic model wiring remain open.
papers.human_summary: Main early results and selected Section 5 bridge endpoints are exposed; Bayesian-game row identifications remain open.

human_review.source: paper-local status.json review_surface; human entries come from dashboard logs

papers.title: College Admissions and the Stability of Marriage
papers.authors: D. Gale and L. S. Shapley
papers.main_caveat: Lean endpoints are closed without extra certificates or conditional proof gaps; the cached PDF scan has poor OCR, recorded as a source-audit note rather than a formalization caveat.
papers.human_summary: This only uses a few lines of code as its infrastructure has largely been elevated to the shared matching library.

papers.title: Marriage, Honesty, and Stability
papers.main_caveat: Matching preliminaries and several theorem wrappers are formalized, but Algorithm 4.1, repaired Lemma 4.4 tail variance, and Section 6 experiment construction remain stronger-model pickup targets.
papers.human_summary: Matching preliminaries and theorem wrappers are exposed; Algorithm 4.1 and experiment constructions remain open.

papers.title: Algorithmic Monoculture and Social Welfare

papers.human_summary: Continuous RUM endpoints are exposed; arbitrary-size Mallows dominance and remaining paper-scope bridges remain open.

papers.title: On Approximately Fair Allocations of Indivisible Goods
papers.authors: Richard J. Lipton, Evangelos Markakis, Elchanan Mossel, and Amin Saberi
papers.main_caveat: Public partial: Sections 2 and 4 are formalized and much of the Section 3 rounding/search surface is formalized. The final PTAS/FPTAS runtime claim is conditional on reusable fixed-dimension integer-program complexity infrastructure, represented by ExternalSolverConsequence.
papers.human_summary: Sections 2 and 4 are closed; Section 3 has query/descent/rounded-search support. The PTAS/FPTAS runtime layer needs reusable fixed-dimension IP complexity infrastructure.

human_review.source: paper-local status.json review_surface; human entries come from dashboard logs

papers.title: Truth Revelation in Approximately Efficient Combinatorial Auctions
papers.authors: Daniel Lehmann, Liadan Ita O'Callaghan, and Yoav Shoham
papers.main_caveat: Public partial: all auction, greedy approximation, critical-price, and single-minded-truthfulness endpoints are formalized in the current model. The final Theorem 6.1 machine-level NP-hardness/NP = ZPP consequences remain conditional on reusable complexity-class and hardness infrastructure.
papers.human_summary: Greedy approximation, truthfulness, and Theorem 6.1 reductions are closed; machine-level complexity remains external.

human_review.source: paper-local status.json review_surface; human entries come from dashboard logs

papers.title: Reconciling the Accuracy-Diversity Trade-off in Recommendations

papers.main_caveat: Finite optimization, Bernoulli, uniform, bounded, exponential, and Pareto checkpoint layers are exposed; general bounded/Pareto order-statistic certificates and Corollary 1 distribution construction remain open.
papers.human_summary: Finite optimization and distribution checkpoints are exposed; general order-statistic certificates remain open.
